title: clerodendrum fortunatum
intro: Clerodendrum fortunatum, also known as the Harsh or Keshu, is a perennial shrub native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
